question_text:
Graph Valid Tree

You have a graph of n nodes labeled from 0 to n - 1. You are given an integer n and a list of edges where edges[i] = [ai, bi] indicates that there is an undirected edge between nodes ai and bi. Return true if the edges of the given graph make up a valid tree, and false otherwise.

Constraints:

  • 1 <= n <= 2000
  • 0 <= edges.length <= 5000
  • edges[i].length == 2
  • All edges are unique.
input_format: Integer n (number of nodes) and array edges [[ai,bi],…].
output_format: true if the edges form a valid tree (connected, no cycle, n-1 edges), false otherwise.
explanation:
Union-Find

A valid tree has exactly n-1 edges and is connected (no cycles). First check if edges.length == n-1. Then use Union-Find: for each edge, if both nodes share the same root, a cycle exists → return false. If all edges merge successfully, return true.

Time: O(N×α(N)) ≈ O(N). Space: O(N).
